2604.09066.title: Anchored Sliding Window: Toward Robust and Imperceptible Linguistic Steganography
2604.09066.paper_summary: Linguistic steganography based on language models typically assumes that steganographic texts are transmitted without alteration, making them fragile to even minor modifications. While previous work mitigates this fragility by limiting the context window, it significantly compromises text quality. In this paper, we propose the anchored sliding window (ASW) framework to improve imperceptibility and robustness. In addition to the latest tokens, the prompt and a bridge context are anchored within the context window, encouraging the model to compensate for the excluded tokens. We formulate the optimization of the bridge context as a variant of prompt distillation, which we further extend using self-distillation strategies. Experiments show that our ASW significantly and consistently outperforms the baseline method in text quality, imperceptibility, and robustness across diverse settings. The code is available at github.com/ryehr/ASW_steganography.
2604.09066.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型基礎上的語言隱寫術通常假設隱寫文本在傳輸過程中不會被改動，這使得它們對於即使是輕微的修改也非常脆弱。雖然以往的研究通過限制上下文窗口來減輕這種脆弱性，但這顯著妥協了文本質量。在本文中，我們提出了錨定滑動窗口（ASW）框架，以提高不可察覺性和穩健性。除了最新的標記外，提示和橋接上下文也被錨定在上下文窗口內，鼓勵模型補償被排除的標記。我們將橋接上下文的優化公式化為提示蒸餾的一種變體，並進一步利用自我蒸餾策略進行擴展。實驗表明，我們的ASW在文本質量、不可察覺性和穩健性方面顯著且持續地超越了基線方法，適用於多種設置。代碼可在github.com/ryehr/ASW_steganography上獲得。
2604.09066.author: Ruiyi Yan et.al.
2604.09066.authors: Ruiyi Yan, Shiao Meng, Yugo Murawaki
